tasks.title: Store bottled drinking water
tasks.description: Keep sealed water ready for immediate use so the household can start a 72-hour plan without queueing or filling containers under pressure.

tasks.title: Fill food-grade water containers before warnings
tasks.description: Top up clean containers early when severe weather or outages are forecast so you are not relying on mains pressure later.

tasks.title: Rotate stored water every 6 months
tasks.description: Replace older water on a fixed schedule so taste, seals, and container condition are checked before an emergency.

tasks.title: Separate drinking water from washing water
tasks.description: Allocate clean water for drinking and cooking first, and keep lower-grade water for cleaning and flushing.

tasks.title: Store dedicated water for pets
tasks.description: Set aside extra water for animals so household drinking reserves are not consumed faster than planned.

tasks.title: Keep grab-and-go water bottles packed
tasks.description: Place small bottles in go-bags and vehicles so each person has immediate water during evacuation or transport delays.

tasks.title: Protect stored water from heat
tasks.description: Store water out of direct sun and away from hot sheds or vehicle cabins so containers last longer and water stays usable.

tasks.title: Keep a water treatment backup
tasks.description: Store tablets, filters, or boiling equipment so you can make uncertain water safer if mains supply is contaminated.

tasks.title: Label non-potable water clearly
tasks.description: Mark utility water so nobody accidentally drinks stored cleaning or collected floodwater.

tasks.title: Set a household water ration
tasks.description: Decide a daily water allocation for each person before you are under stress so reserves last the full plan duration.

tasks.title: Learn how to isolate the home water supply
tasks.description: Know where the main valve is so you can shut down damaged plumbing or contaminated inflow safely.

tasks.title: Stage a vehicle water cache
tasks.description: Keep extra water in a durable carrier for evacuations, breakdowns, or long detours.

tasks.title: Build a 3-day food supply
tasks.description: Store enough ready-to-eat food for at least 72 hours so the household can shelter safely when shops or roads are disrupted.

tasks.title: Build a 7-day pantry buffer
tasks.description: Expand beyond the minimum and keep a week of shelf-stable food to cover transport delays and extended outages.

tasks.title: Store no-cook meals
tasks.description: Keep food that can be eaten cold if power, gas, or safe cooking space is unavailable.

tasks.title: Rotate pantry expiry dates
tasks.description: Use and replace stored food through normal meals so emergency food stays familiar and in date.

tasks.title: Keep a manual can opener with stored food
tasks.description: Avoid relying on electric kitchen gear during outages and make canned food immediately usable.

tasks.title: Store infant and special-diet food
tasks.description: Keep enough formula, texture-modified meals, or prescribed nutrition products for anyone who depends on them.

tasks.title: Separate allergy-safe emergency food
tasks.description: Store clearly labelled food that avoids known triggers so nobody improvises unsafe meals under pressure.

tasks.title: Plan fridge-outage food use
tasks.description: Know what to eat first and when chilled food is no longer safe so waste and illness risk are reduced.

tasks.title: Pack vehicle rations
tasks.description: Keep compact food in the car in case detours, fuel queues, or road closures turn a short trip into a long wait.

tasks.title: Protect stored food from heat and pests
tasks.description: Use sealed containers and cooler storage areas so food quality holds through summer and storm season.

tasks.title: Keep familiar comfort food and caffeine options
tasks.description: A small reserve of familiar items helps sustain morale and prevents sudden caffeine withdrawal headaches.

tasks.title: Store quick hydration snacks
tasks.description: Keep salty, easy-to-eat food that supports hydration and energy when appetite is low or heat is high.

tasks.title: Assemble a household first aid kit
tasks.description: Keep a complete first aid kit where everyone can access it quickly, and review missing items twice a year.

tasks.title: Hold a prescription medicine buffer
tasks.description: Work with normal refill timing so critical medicines are not down to the last dose when services are disrupted.

tasks.title: Record medical conditions and allergies
tasks.description: Keep a concise written record so helpers can act safely if phones are flat or the patient cannot speak clearly.

tasks.title: Keep spare glasses and hearing aid supplies
tasks.description: Store vision and hearing backups so mobility and communication do not drop sharply after damage or evacuation.

tasks.title: Pack pain relief and oral rehydration salts
tasks.description: Keep simple treatment for pain, fever, and dehydration in both the home kit and evacuation bag.

tasks.title: Learn CPR and bleeding control
tasks.description: Practice core lifesaving skills so the household can stabilise a casualty while waiting for help.

tasks.title: Stock burn dressings
tasks.description: Keep appropriate dressings and gel to manage minor burns from cooking, generators, or radiant heat.

tasks.title: Prepare pet medical supplies
tasks.description: Store animal medications, wound care items, and handling gear alongside household medical supplies.

tasks.title: Check medicine storage temperature limits
tasks.description: Move heat-sensitive medicines to safer storage during hot weather or outages so they remain effective.

tasks.title: Keep hygiene supplies for 72 hours
tasks.description: Store soap, wipes, sanitary items, and waste bags so illness risk stays lower during disrupted services.

tasks.title: Store masks, gloves, and disinfectant
tasks.description: Keep basic protective items ready for smoke, dirty cleanup work, and contaminated surfaces.

tasks.title: Prepare spare mobility and battery aids
tasks.description: Keep backup chargers, batteries, or mobility essentials for anyone who depends on assistive equipment.

tasks.title: Save emergency contacts offline
tasks.description: Keep critical contact numbers on the device and on paper so they remain available without signal or power.

tasks.title: Agree on an out-of-area contact
tasks.description: Choose one person outside the hazard zone who can relay updates if local networks are congested.

tasks.title: Program and test the emergency radio
tasks.description: Pre-set local stations and test reception so warnings can be monitored quickly during outages.

tasks.title: Print local emergency broadcast frequencies
tasks.description: Keep station lists with your radio kit so anyone in the household can tune in without searching online.

tasks.title: Download offline maps and alert apps
tasks.description: Store maps and official warning apps ahead of time so key information is available when signal is weak.

tasks.title: Teach children key phone numbers
tasks.description: Practice the numbers they may need if they become separated or borrow another person’s phone.

tasks.title: Practice short status messages
tasks.description: Agree on concise messages such as location, safety, and next move so limited battery and signal are used well.

tasks.title: Keep a laminated contact sheet
tasks.description: Store names, numbers, addresses, and medical notes in a waterproof sleeve with go-bags and the car.

tasks.title: Set a family check-in schedule
tasks.description: Agree on regular times to report in so silence is noticed early and battery use stays predictable.

tasks.title: Enable location sharing when signal exists
tasks.description: Know how to switch on trusted location sharing quickly when moving or evacuating.

tasks.title: Keep a whistle signal plan
tasks.description: Use simple whistle patterns for help, regrouping, or location marking where voices may not carry.

tasks.title: Label all device chargers and cables
tasks.description: Avoid wasting time matching cables in the dark and keep power options ready for phones and radios.

tasks.title: Charge power banks before risk days
tasks.description: Top up battery packs when warnings are issued so you start the event with full reserve power.

tasks.title: Keep spare batteries sorted by size
tasks.description: Store fresh batteries by type and label opened packs so torches and radios can be re-powered fast.

tasks.title: Test torches monthly
tasks.description: Switch on every torch and replace weak batteries before an emergency exposes failed gear.

tasks.title: Plan refrigeration backup for food and medicine
tasks.description: Decide what goes in the esky first and how long chilled items can stay safe if power is lost.

tasks.title: Store car USB chargers
tasks.description: Keep vehicle charging available so phones and lights can be topped up while parked or evacuating.

tasks.title: Stage a small solar charger
tasks.description: Keep a compact solar panel or charger ready for multi-day outages or off-grid travel.

tasks.title: Learn generator safety
tasks.description: Operate generators outdoors, well clear of doors and windows, and protect cords from rain and trip hazards.

tasks.title: Protect critical devices with surge boards
tasks.description: Use surge protection for chargers and essential electronics when unstable power returns.

tasks.title: Keep headlamps beside beds
tasks.description: Hands-free lighting reduces falls and lets people move safely during sudden night-time outages.

tasks.title: Reduce non-essential power draw early
tasks.description: Shut down unneeded devices to preserve battery banks, generators, and vehicle charging time.

tasks.title: Keep a backup cooking method
tasks.description: Store safe outdoor or ventilated cooking gear so meals remain possible without mains power.

tasks.title: Plan backup power for medical devices
tasks.description: Identify charging, battery, or relocation options for any essential medical equipment in the home.

tasks.title: Choose a primary evacuation route
tasks.description: Pick the fastest safe route and write it down so nobody is deciding under time pressure.

tasks.title: Choose a secondary evacuation route
tasks.description: Keep an alternate road or direction ready in case your main route is cut or congested.

tasks.title: Stage go-bags by the main exit
tasks.description: Store ready bags near the door so people can move quickly with medications, documents, and water.

tasks.title: Copy documents into a waterproof pouch
tasks.description: Keep IDs, insurance details, and medical information protected from rain, floodwater, and smoke.

tasks.title: Identify local shelter options
tasks.description: Know where to go if home is unsafe, including official centres and family or friends outside the risk area.

tasks.title: Set a pet evacuation plan
tasks.description: Prepare carriers, leads, food, and accommodation options so animals can move with the household.

tasks.title: Plan school and work pickup roles
tasks.description: Decide who collects children or dependants if roads close or normal routines break down.

tasks.title: Keep cash in small notes
tasks.description: Cash helps when card terminals are offline or fuel and food outlets are limiting transactions.

tasks.title: Refuel vehicles at half a tank
tasks.description: Avoid waiting until the last quarter so evacuation and supply runs remain viable during queues or shortages.

tasks.title: Practice a 10-minute departure
tasks.description: Run a timed drill so the household knows exactly what to grab and who does each task.

tasks.title: Photograph key household assets
tasks.description: Record rooms, equipment, and serial numbers to support insurance and recovery after damage.

tasks.title: Plan neighbour check-ins
tasks.description: Identify who may need extra warning or transport assistance and agree how you will check on them safely.

tasks.title: Clear gutters and roof valleys
tasks.description: Remove leaf litter and debris that can ignite or block drainage before fire and storm season.

tasks.title: Trim fuel around the home
tasks.description: Reduce long grass, dry leaf build-up, and low branches close to the house to lower ember and flame exposure.

tasks.title: Test smoke alarms monthly
tasks.description: Check alarms, replace weak batteries, and confirm everyone can hear them from sleeping areas.

tasks.title: Place extinguishers near kitchen and garage
tasks.description: Keep extinguishers where fire risk is higher and teach adults when to use them and when to leave.

tasks.title: Store P2 masks for smoke events
tasks.description: Keep masks sealed and ready for outdoor movement, cleanup, or driving through smoky conditions.

tasks.title: Keep hoses connected and reachable
tasks.description: Stage hoses where they can be used quickly for spot fires, cooling, or ember cleanup if safe to stay.

tasks.title: Move flammables away from the house
tasks.description: Store gas bottles, fuel, kindling, and spare timber away from walls and access paths.

tasks.title: Check fire blankets and access points
tasks.description: Make sure blankets are mounted, accessible, and not blocked by storage in kitchens or garages.

tasks.title: Prepare ember patrol tools
tasks.description: Keep gloves, torch, ladder access, and a rake or shovel ready for spot fires and post-front checks.

tasks.title: Seal gaps against embers
tasks.description: Screen vents and close gaps where embers can enter roofs, decks, and underfloor spaces.

tasks.title: Review local fire danger ratings
tasks.description: Check ratings and warnings routinely during fire season so you act early instead of reacting late.

tasks.title: Plan safe cooking during outages
tasks.description: Choose safe cooking locations and keep open flames away from vehicles, tents, and combustible surfaces.

tasks.title: Move valuables above the expected flood line
tasks.description: Pre-stage critical items on higher shelves or an upper floor before heavy rain arrives.

tasks.title: Mark a safe high-ground route
tasks.description: Know the quickest way to higher ground that does not rely on low crossings or creek roads.

tasks.title: Store sandbags and plastic sheeting
tasks.description: Keep simple flood barriers ready so doorways and low vents can be protected early.

tasks.title: Learn mains power shutdown for floods
tasks.description: Know how to cut power safely before water enters the building or garage.

tasks.title: Commit to never driving through floodwater
tasks.description: Set the rule before the event so the household does not take risks with unknown depth or road damage.

tasks.title: Protect documents in a waterproof bag
tasks.description: Keep papers sealed in a grab-and-go pouch so they survive rain, mud, and quick evacuations.

tasks.title: Secure outdoor items before heavy rain
tasks.description: Move loose items that can block drains, damage windows, or float into access paths.

tasks.title: Seal chemical and fuel storage
tasks.description: Reduce contamination by securing chemicals, paints, fuels, and cleaners above likely flood reach.

tasks.title: Inspect drains and grates before storms
tasks.description: Clear obvious blockages around the home so runoff can move away more effectively.

tasks.title: Pack cleanup disinfectant and gloves
tasks.description: Keep simple cleanup supplies ready for dirty water, mud, and post-flood hygiene work.

tasks.title: Prepare an after-flood hygiene plan
tasks.description: Plan safe waste handling, hand washing, and laundry until normal water and sewer systems return.

tasks.title: Check water quality after flooding
tasks.description: Use stored water until authorities confirm safety or treat water with your backup method.

tasks.title: Keep the vehicle serviced for evacuation
tasks.description: Address basic maintenance so the car is less likely to fail during an urgent departure or long detour.

tasks.title: Store a usable spare tyre and tools
tasks.description: Check the spare, jack, and wheel brace before long trips or hazard season.

tasks.title: Keep a jump starter charged
tasks.description: Maintain a charged jump starter so a flat battery does not strand the household during an emergency.

tasks.title: Carry a recovery strap on remote routes
tasks.description: Pack basic recovery gear when travelling on soft ground or isolated roads.

tasks.title: Pack water and food in the vehicle
tasks.description: Keep vehicle reserves for delays, road closures, or breakdowns away from services.

tasks.title: Keep a paper map in the vehicle
tasks.description: Store a local or state map in the glovebox in case data coverage, power, or navigation apps fail.

tasks.title: Maintain a vehicle first aid kit
tasks.description: Keep a small but complete kit in the car for breakdowns, travel, and evacuations.

tasks.title: Store a window breaker and seatbelt cutter
tasks.description: Place rescue tools where the driver can reach them without searching.

tasks.title: Keep a spare phone charger in the vehicle
tasks.description: Maintain charging access during long waits, detours, or response trips.

tasks.title: Check wipers before storm season
tasks.description: Replace worn blades before visibility becomes a safety issue in heavy rain.

tasks.title: Keep two route options for key trips
tasks.description: Know at least one alternate route for school, work, and evacuation travel.

tasks.title: Log remote trip intentions with someone trusted
tasks.description: Tell a trusted contact where you are going, which route you are taking, and when to expect a check-in.

tasks.title: Assign household emergency roles
tasks.description: Decide who handles pets, documents, children, first aid, and vehicle loading so no critical task is missed.

tasks.title: Rehearse the children’s reunion plan
tasks.description: Practice where children wait, who picks them up, and which backup adult can step in.

tasks.title: Choose a neighbour support contact
tasks.description: Pick one nearby person to share quick updates or help with checks when families are split between work and home.

tasks.title: Maintain a contact card for each member
tasks.description: Give each person a small card with names, numbers, addresses, and allergies.

tasks.title: Note pet feeding and handling instructions
tasks.description: Write down food, medications, carrier setup, and calming tips for anyone who may move or care for the pet.

tasks.title: Share key and alarm info with a trusted person
tasks.description: Make sure one trusted person can access the home if you are away or delayed.

tasks.title: Set medication responsibility
tasks.description: Decide who checks, packs, and carries each critical medication during evacuation or sheltering.

tasks.title: Update family photos for identification
tasks.description: Keep recent photos available to help if someone is separated during a large-scale event.

tasks.title: Choose a phone-free meeting rule
tasks.description: Agree where to wait and how long to stay even if phones fail or batteries are flat.

tasks.title: Practice a blackout evening
tasks.description: Run an evening without mains power so lighting, cooking, charging, and family routines are tested in reality.

tasks.title: Confirm school and work emergency contacts
tasks.description: Check that organisations have the right names and numbers to reach the household in a crisis.

tasks.title: Review the household plan every season
tasks.description: Refresh tasks, supplies, routes, and contact details before conditions change.
